file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: Now let me add the two new types.

input.new_string:
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domains, subdomains, IP
# addresses, ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
url = Regex(
    r"https?:\/\/(?:www\.)?[-a-zA-Z0-9@:%._\+~#=]{1,256}\.[a-zA-Z0-9()]{1,6}\b(?:[-a-zA-Z0-9()@:%_\+.~#?&\/\/=]*)"
)

# Matches a SHA-256 hash represented as 64 hexadecimal characters.
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domains, subdomains, IP
+# addresses, ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash represented as 64 hexadecimal characters.

content.text: Now let's verify with a quick sanity test.

input.command:
python3 -c "
from outlines import types
import re

url_tests = [
    ('http://example.com', True),
    ('https://example.com', True),
    ('https://www.example.com/path?query=1#frag', True),
    ('https://sub.example.co.uk:8080/path', True),
    ('https://192.168.1.1', True),
    ('ftp://example.com', False),
    ('not a url', False),
    ('https://exa mple.com', False),
]
for s, expected in url_tests:
    m = types.url.matches(s)
    print(s, m, expected, 'OK' if bool(m)==expected else 'FAIL')

hash_tests = [
    ('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'[:64], True),
    ('a'*64, True),
    ('A'*64, True),
    ('a'*63, False),
    ('a'*65, False),
    ('g'*64, False),
    ('', False),
]
for s, expected in hash_tests:
    m = types.hash_sha256.matches(s)
    print(repr(s)[:20], m, expected, 'OK' if bool(m)==expected else 'FAIL')
"

"

python3 -c "
from outlines import types
import re

url_tests = [
    ('http://example.com', True),
    ('https://example.com', True),
    ('https://www.example.com/path?query=1#frag', True),
    ('https://sub.example.co.uk:8080/path', True),
    ('https://192.168.1.1', True),
    ('ftp://example.com', False),
    ('not a url', False),
    ('https://exa mple.com', False),
]
for s, expected in url_tests:
    m = types.url.matches(s)
    print(s, m, expected, 'OK' if bool(m)==expected else 'FAIL')

hash_tests = [
    ('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'[:64], True),
    ('a'*64, True),
    ('A'*64, True),
    ('a'*63, False),
    ('a'*65, False),
    ('g'*64, False),
    ('', False),
]
for s, expected in hash_tests:
    m = types.hash_sha256.matches(s)
    print(repr(s)[:20], m, expected, 'OK' if bool(m)==expected else 'FAIL')
"

content.text: All checks pass. Let's also test pydantic integration and `.validate()` method quickly.

input.command:
python3 -c "
from outlines import types
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Model(BaseModel):
    website: types.url
    checksum: types.hash_sha256

m = Model(website='https://example.com', checksum='a'*64)
print(m)
print(types.url.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))
print(types.hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))

try:
    Model(website='not a url', checksum='a'*64)
    print('FAIL: should have raised')
except Exception as e:
    print('OK raised:', type(e).__name__)
"

content.text: Everything works as expected. Now let's generate the patch.

content.content:
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..2e65b1cc 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,12 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches HTTP and HTTPS web URLs, including domains, subdomains, IP
+# addresses, ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
+url = Regex(
+    r"https?:\/\/(?:www\.)?[-a-zA-Z0-9@:%._\+~#=]{1,256}\.[a-zA-Z0-9()]{1,6}\b(?:[-a-zA-Z0-9()@:%_\+.~#?&\/\/=]*)"
+)
+
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash represented as 64 hexadecimal characters.
+hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

+# addresses, ports, paths, query parameters and fragments.
+url = Regex(
+    r"https?:\/\/(?:www\.)?[-a-zA-Z0-9@:%._\+~#=]{1,256}\.[a-zA-Z0-9()]{1,6}\b(?:[-a-zA-Z0-9()@:%_\+.~#?&\/\/=]*)"
+)
+
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash represented as 64 hexadecimal characters.
+hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")

content.text:
Added two new regex-based types to `outlines/types/__init__.py`:

- **`url`** — validates HTTP/HTTPS URLs (domains, subdomains, IPs, ports, paths, query strings, fragments)
- **`hash_sha256`** — validates 64-character hex SHA-256 hashes (case-insensitive)

Both integrate automatically with the existing `Regex` class (Pydantic schema generation, `.matches()`, `.validate()`, DSL operations) since they're just `Regex` instances like `email`/`isbn`. Verified with manual tests covering valid/invalid URLs, edge-length hashes, and Pydantic model validation. Diff written to `patch.txt`.
